The lecture slide is titled "What is 'self' supervision?" and is part of a Deep Learning course at NYU, Spring 2020. The slide explains the concept of self-supervision in machine learning, which involves:

1. **Obtain "labels" from the data itself by using a "semi-automatic" process**: This means that instead of manually labeling data, the labels are derived from the data itself through a semi-automatic process.

2. **Predict part of the data from other parts**: This refers to the ability of a model to predict certain parts of the data based on other parts of the same data
